move.claim: Let n = Ï(N). Then log n â¼ log N, log log n â¼ log log N, log log log n â¼ log log log N, log log log log n â¼ log log log log N as N â â.

dependencies: Prime Number Theorem: Ï(N) â¼ li(N) â¼ N / log N
move.expected_effect: Bridges parameter expressions between interval length L(N) and target gap in terms of n.
move.why_this_applies_here: No direct prior case match; standard asymptotic normalization in analytic number theory, akin to size estimates in Euclid's proof but for logs.

move.claim: The target statement holds if, for any C>0, there are infinitely many N with no primes in (N, N+L] where L = c log N (log log N log log log log N) / (log log log N)^2 for c = c(C)>0 sufficiently small.
move.rationale: If Ï(N+L)=Ï(N)=n, then p_{n+1} - p_n > L by definition. With the log asymptotic lemma, L â¼ target expression in n; small c absorbs PNT errors and higher-order terms.
move.test_plan: Lean formalization: prove implication Ï(N+L)=Ï(N) â gap > L; symbolically check L(N)/target(n) â constant >0 using log expansions; use crude PNT bounds Ï(N) < 2N/log N to bound errors.
move.expected_effect: Achieves Milestone 1, reducing to prime-free interval construction.
move.risk: medium (prior similar move timed out; mitigate with crude bounds, no full PNT formalization)
move.why_this_applies_here: Mirrors Euclid (sample_solved_001): construct N with forced property (new prime there via product+1; here prime-free via modular covering); transfers constructive infinitude pattern.
move.how_to_falsify_fast: If log n â° log N - log log N + O(1), check PNT counterexample; or if gap â¤ L for some explicit N,L via prime tables.

move.claim: Given distinct primes q_1,...,q_k (hence pairwise coprime), residues a_i mod q_i, and H such that {1,...,H} â âª_i (a_i + q_i â¤), let Q=â q_i and a solve CRT: x â¡ -h mod q_i for each covering h. Then for infinitely many N â¡ a mod Q with N > max(Q, max q_i^2), the interval (N, N+H] is prime-free.
move.rationale: For each h=1..H, N+h â¡ 0 mod q_{i(h)}, so q_{i(h)} | N+h; since N+h > q_i^2 â¥ 4, and q_i prime, N+h composite. Infinitely many such N by density 1/Q >0 in the AP.
move.test_plan: Lean: formalize covering condition â compositeness for large N; check trivial AP infinitude.
dependencies: CRT solvability for pairwise coprime moduli
move.expected_effect: Links covering systems to infinitely many prime-free intervals, advancing to parameter choice.
move.why_this_applies_here: Extends Euclid (sample_solved_001) construction: product-based modulus forces composites in interval (vs new prime); same infinitude via AP.
move.how_to_falsify_fast: Test small covering e.g. q1=2 a1=1 (odds), but incomplete; manually check if any h uncovered or N+h prime for small N.
